rationale: The citing paper places Deleersnyder et al. (2009) within the core business-cycle stream it builds from and contrasts against when motivating an intrayear-cycle focus. It also cites that work in the methods section as prior precedent for using cycle-extraction/comovement style analysis to let data define cycles and study marketing responses. This is more than a passing background mention because it supports both the paper's positioning and key modeling choices, even though the citation appears in grouped references.
relation_description: The citing study applies the business-cycle cycle-analysis tradition represented by Deleersnyder et al. (2009) to a different temporal setting: intrayear category-demand cycles and brand-level advertising/pricing effectiveness. It transfers the prior cyclical-comovement logic rather than testing the cited paper's country-culture claims directly.
